highlights.title: OpenAI secures U.S. regulatory green light for GPT-5.6 rollout, Axios report says
highlights.description: The White House told CNBC that it did not give OpenAI a "green light," and such decisions "rest entirely with the companies."

highlights.title: China warns about AI risks with Anthropic's Claude Code
highlights.description: China said specific versions of Claude Code posed back-door vulnerabilities that could send sensitive information to a remote server.

highlights.title: Ispace CEO on Lunar Transportation Plans With SpaceX
highlights.description: Takeshi Hakamada, Founder and CEO at Ispace, discusses the company's plans to to create a lunar payload service to be launched aboard a SpaceX Starship rocket. He speaks with Shery Ahn on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: China Chipmaker CXMT to Launch $4.3 Billion IPO Next Week
highlights.description: Memory giant CXMT Corp. will open investor subscriptions for its public offering next week, kicking off the final stage of China’s most highly anticipated listing of the year.

highlights.title: AirPods-maker Luxshare sees shares close lower in tepid Hong Kong debut
highlights.description: The company, which is already listed in Shenzhen, had priced shares in the IPO at 63.28 Hong Kong dollars apiece, raising HK$24.27 billion ($3.09 billion).

highlights.title: World Cup drives Google Search to record queries per second
highlights.description: The milestone comes amid Lionel Messi and Argentina's popularity.

highlights.title: IMF Raises S. Korea Growth Most Among Major Economies on AI Boom
highlights.description: South Korea’s growth outlook received the biggest upgrade among the world’s 30 major economies in the International Monetary Fund’s latest projections, adding to evidence that the country’s artificial intelligence-driven semiconductor boom is overpowering the economic hit from the Middle East conflict.

highlights.title: Two blockbuster AI IPO's on the horizon could leave crypto further behind
highlights.description: A wave of blockbuster AI listings is drawing billions of dollars from global investors, reinforcing a rotation away from crypto.

highlights.title: AI Capex Cycle Hasn't Reached Overspend Yet: BlackRock
highlights.description: Helen Jewell, BlackRock's fundamental equities international CIO, discusses investment in the artificial intelligence space, saying "the next few years are very clear from an AI perspective because of the amount of capital that you'll see being invested in that space." Speaking on Bloomberg Television, Jewell adds that while every capex cycle reaches the point when overspend starts to happen, that is not the case yet. "We don't believe that we're there now." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: China’s Reflation Shows Signs of Peaking as War Shock Fades
highlights.description: China’s reflationary momentum showed signs of stalling in June, a reminder that the outlook for domestic prices is fragile as the economy emerges from deflation after an easing of tensions over Iran led to a pullback in commodity costs.

ai.title: Recruiters Shift Focus to Specialized AI Jobs to Stay Relevant
ai.description: Recruitment companies, facing the threat of artificial intelligence tools replacing human labor and customers embracing AI-assisted applicant screening, are adapting by honing in on niche, in-demand jobs in the AI economy.

ai.title: nubia Neo 5 GT Special Edition debuts with the first and only Liquid and Air Dual Active Cooling System in its class
ai.description: PARTNER CONTENT: nubia aims to democratize premium gaming with a revolutionary AquaCore thermal architecture, launching first in Southeast Asia

chips_cloud.title: Ubuntu emphasizes Arm64 support – and gets Rustier
chips_cloud.description: Even so, it has some issues with the Rust coreutils

chips_cloud.title: Microsoft shifts to annual exchange rate price revision for cloudy products
chips_cloud.description: Previously did it twice a year, so customers now have fewer chances to surf FX waves
